title: Flour Tortillas

overview.description: Soft homemade flour tortillas that are perfect for fajitas, enchiladas, burritos and wraps. Simple to make and far superior to shop-bought versions.

method: Combine the flour and salt in a large bowl.
Rub the lard into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs.
Gradually add the warm water, mixing until a dough forms.
=== Knead ===
Knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
=== Rest ===
Cover the dough and leave to rest for 30 minutes.
=== Shape ===
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball.
Cover and rest for 10 minutes.
Roll each ball into a thin circle approximately 20–22cm across.
=== Cook ===
Heat a dry cast iron pan or heavy frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Cook each tortilla for 30–60 seconds on the first side until bubbles appear.
Turn and cook for a further 20–40 seconds.
Avoid overcooking to keep the tortillas soft.
=== Cool ===
Stack the cooked tortillas inside a clean tea towel to keep them warm and soft.

learning.did-you-know: Flour tortillas originated in northern Mexico, where wheat grows more readily than maize.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How fat affects dough texture, how resting relaxes gluten and how to cook flatbreads quickly on a hot pan.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Homemade tortillas transform Mexican meals and cost a fraction of shop-bought packs.
chapman-tips: Keep the dough covered whenever you're not working with it.
A hot pan cooks the tortilla before it dries out.
Stack cooked tortillas under a tea towel to keep them flexible.
Freeze with baking paper between each tortilla for easy separation.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in stacks once completely cool.
notes.leftovers: Use for wraps, quesadillas or homemade tortilla chips.
